preFix.title: Replacing a pendant lamp holder
preFix.subtitle: Swap out a cracked or heat-damaged lamp holder on a ceiling pendant
preFix.excerpt: How to replace a lamp holder on a ceiling pendant: isolating the circuit, disconnecting at the ceiling rose, fitting the new holder, and restoring the supply.

content.text: that is cracked, heat-discoloured, or has a seized lamp socket is a fire risk and should be replaced. The job is simple once the circuit is isolated: the flex disconnects from the ceiling rose, the old holder unscrews, and the new holder screws on.

Switch off the lighting circuit MCB and fit the lock-off device. Test the ceiling rose terminals are dead with the two-pole tester. If you are unsure which MCB controls the circuit, switch each in turn until the light goes out.
Step 2, disconnect the flex at the rose

Unscrew and lower the ceiling rose cover. Note which terminals the flex conductors connect to (photograph before disconnecting). Loosen the terminal screws and withdraw the flex conductors. Release the cord grip at the top of the rose that holds the flex. Lower the pendant.

Unscrew the skirt from the old lamp holder and slide off the old holder. Thread the flex through the skirt of the new holder. Connect the flex conductors to the new holder terminals: the blue (neutral) connects to the terminal linked to the screw thread of an ES holder, and the brown (live) connects to the centre contact. Tighten the
on the outer flex sheath before tightening the terminal screws, so the weight of the holder rests on the grip rather than on the wire connections. Screw the skirt closed.
Step 4, reconnect at the rose and test

Pass the flex through the ceiling rose cord grip and reconnect the conductors to the same terminals they came from. Set the cord grip in the rose to take the flex weight. Refit the rose cover. Remove the lock-off device, restore the circuit, and test the lamp.

attrs.fix: Isolate again and check all four terminal connections (two at the rose, two at the holder). An ES holder with live and neutral reversed will function but the lamp shell is live; reverse them.
attrs.cause: A conductor is not fully tightened in a terminal, or the live and neutral are reversed at the ES holder.
attrs.symptom: Lamp flickers or does not light after replacement
